puzzle0.title: IDENTITY CORRELATION
puzzle0.description: Match each recovered fragment with the correct identity clue.

puzzle0.title: MEMORY RECONSTRUCTION
puzzle0.description: Use the recovered identity clues to reconstruct the missing crew member.

event1.text: He said you already saw the name.

event10.text: Because my brain keeps poking it like a bruise.

event2.text: Which means either the archive is hiding it, or one of us walked past it without realizing.

event18.text: A name you saw and forgot is worse.

event21.text: That means the damage isn't only in the archive.

event4.text: I want to say more than no.

event6.text: I want to give you something useful. A face. A habit. A stupid nickname.

event1.text: And before you decide that makes me useless, memory is exactly what someone attacked here.

event12.text: Records can be damaged. Memories can be shaped.

event2.text: So let's use records. Records lie less emotionally.

event1.title: CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT
event1.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
STATUS: ERASED
SURNAME: V---
MEMORY INDEX: DAMAGED
SOURCE CONFLICT: OFFICIAL MANIFEST DENIES ENTRY
event1.caption: Partially recovered crew record from corrupted personnel database.
event1.message: [CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: I don't remember anyone with a V surname on ECHO.

event9.text: Grant, Rivera, Park, Owens, Hale, me.

event11.text: I can say the six names without thinking.

event15.text: If there was a seventh, the silence around them is too clean.

event1.text: That file was deleted for a reason.

event2.text: And no, before Maya says it, not because I'm secretly dramatic.

event7.text: Although I admit the evidence for that keeps accumulating.

event10.text: There are names on ECHO that do more than identify people.

event14.text: Sometimes things that should stay closed.

event1.text: You ask me for help, then keep digging the moment I tell you the ground is unstable.

event7.text: But when the floor answers, don't pretend it was only a floor.

event0.text: Because names are not just labels in this system.

event1.text: A name can wake permissions. Doors. Dead processes. Things we shut down for a reason.

event5.text: People think identity systems are boring.

event7.text: They are, until one of them starts treating a ghost like authorized personnel.

event2.text: If this is the name I think it is, you need to be careful.

event12.text: And yes, I hate how little that helps.

event0.title: MAINTENANCE LOG
event0.content:
Relay inspection completed.
Signal bleed detected on emergency carrier.
Manual reset recommended.

Signed: N. Voss
event0.caption: Recovered relay maintenance entry with partial signature.

event2.text: No. I would remember that signature.

event7.text: Maintenance logs passed through Rivera first.

event11.text: She once rejected a report because Park wrote 'weird noise' instead of identifying the noise.

event3.text: Noah Voss wasn't supposed to be in that database.

event5.text: I know the absence of it.

event24.text: It's also the only accurate one.

event1.text: Or someone I was told we didn't bring aboard. That distinction is starting to feel expensive.

event8.text: There is always paperwork before something unforgivable.

event10.text: Transfer denied. Clearance conflict. Do not assign to ECHO.

event12.text: I remember the wording because it was too clean.

event14.text: Clean wording is where institutions hide knives.

event0.text: Good. Don't let either of us narrate this for you.

event1.text: Make the records talk to each other.

event5.text: People defend themselves. Records just contradict each other until something breaks.

event7.text: Today, that might be the closest thing we have to honesty.
Or it wants whatever answers to that name.

event1.text: No. V surname, N. Voss signature, already seen.

event4.text: Put those together, but don't invent more than the records give us.

event6.text: That's how erased people get erased twice.

event2.text: Crew seven. Surname Voss. First initial N.

event3.text: Now reconstruct the missing identity. And be careful. Hale wasn't wrong about names waking things.

event1.text: Which, I realize, has become my entire personality. But I mean it this time.

event2.text: If the system asks for a name, give it exactly what the records support. Nothing extra.

event10.text: And some keys turn from the other side.

event2.text: Wrong reconstruction. The archive didn't like that.

event8.text: N. Voss. Communications specialist. The missing name is not subtle anymore.

event1.title: NOAH VOSS
event1.content:
NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
CLEARANCE LEVEL: OMEGA
STATUS: UNKNOWN
TRANSFER STATUS: DENIED
MEMORY INDEX: SUPPRESSED
event1.caption: Reconstructed personnel profile from fragmented archive records.
event1.message: [NOAH VOSS PROFILE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event5.text: There is a silence after the file opens.

event9.text: Just three people realizing a deleted man has a name again.

event1.text: That shouldn't be possible. I know I keep saying that. I would love for one thing today to be possible.

event9.text: The first person erased from the manifest was the one whose job was to make sure people could speak.

event1.text: Transfer denied. Clearance sealed. Communications specialist nobody wanted to talk about.

event2.text: That is not the same as knowing him.

event8.text: I remember a folder with his name on it.

event10.text: I remember being told not to ask why it was sealed.

event14.text: I do not remember the answer.

event0.text: Because Noah Voss never boarded the station.

event1.text: His transfer request was denied weeks before launch.

event2.text: I remember Rivera making a joke about it. Something about communications specialists being terrible at communicating with HR.

event8.text: She laughed at her own joke.

event12.text: So yes. I remember the denied transfer.

event16.text: Which means the archive isn't only contradicting records.

event0.title: TRANSFER RECORD: NOAH VOSS
event0.content:
TRANSFER REQUEST: NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
STATUS: DENIED
DATE: 21 DAYS BEFORE LAUNCH
REASON: OMEGA CLEARANCE CONFLICT
FINAL NOTE: DO NOT ASSIGN TO ECHO
event0.caption: Recovered transfer record showing Noah Voss was officially denied station assignment.
event0.message: [NOAH VOSS TRANSFER RECORD ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event7.text: Then why is his name inside our station?

event9.text: Not where they let you remember me.

event3.text: I was the first voice you heard.

event18.text: Before the station learned how to pretend silence was safety.

event1.text: Manifest fragment gives us a surname shape: V---.

event3.text: Maintenance signature gives us N. Voss.

event5.text: Unknown said we already saw it.

event8.text: So we don't have a full person yet.

event10.text: We have the outline of someone the archive tried to cut out.

event1.text: The archive has already given us the shape.

event7.text: The reconstructed profile wants the full name.

event12.text: I wish saying it felt less like opening a hatch.
